summary:
A magnitude 7.8 earthquake struck the southern Philippines today, killing at least 31 people and injuring more than 130. The quake hit Mindanao, triggering small tsunami waves and causing widespread damage. Rescue teams are searching for scores still missing, and disaster experts warn the event highlights the vulnerability of Metro Manila to a major quake. 

Geopolitical tensions escalated as Iran and Israel exchanged strikes, collapsing a two-month ceasefire. President Trump called for an immediate ceasefire but maintained the Hormuz blockade. In a separate move, Chinese President Xi Jinping arrived in Pyongyang for a rare summit, pledging unwavering support for North Korea's Kim Jong-un. The visit aims to strengthen their alliance against the United States.

Armenia's election results confirmed a westward shift: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's Civil Contract party won 49.81% of the vote, cementing his pro-Western stance despite Moscow's threats. Ukrainian President Zelensky met with European allies to outline five conditions for peace talks with Russia, as US attention shifts to the Iran conflict. In business news, a Goldman Sachs-backed firm priced Japan's largest 2026 IPO at 2,400 yen per share. Meanwhile, Nigeria's army rescued 360 hostages from a Boko Haram mountain hideout, highlighting the ongoing kidnapping crisis. 

South Africa adopted a comprehensive plan to tackle illegal immigration, focusing on border security and closing policy loopholes. Peruvians voted in a runoff election for their ninth president in a decade, choosing between rightwing Keiko Fujimori and leftist Roberto Sánchez amid rising crime and political instability.

headlines.title: Magnitude 7.8 earthquake kills at least 31 in southern Philippines
headlines.summary: A 7.8-magnitude earthquake struck Mindanao, killing at least 31, injuring over 130, and triggering small tsunami waves. Rescue operations continue with scores missing.

framings.angle: Emphasized disaster planning lessons for Metro Manila
Focused on casualties and visual impact of collapsed buildings
Reported tsunami warnings across multiple countries

headlines.title: Xi Jinping pledges unwavering support for North Korea during Pyongyang visit
headlines.summary: Chinese President Xi Jinping arrived in Pyongyang for a rare two-day summit, pledging strong support for Kim Jong-un. The visit aims to strengthen their alliance against the US.

framings.angle: Quoted Xi's commitment to traditional friendship
Analyzed mutual strategic interests against the West
Noted high stakes and ramifications for bilateral ties

headlines.title: Iran and Israel exchange strikes as two-month ceasefire collapses
headlines.summary: Iran launched missiles at Israel, which struck military targets in Iran, breaking an uneasy truce. President Trump called for an immediate ceasefire but maintained the Hormuz blockade.

framings.angle: Questioned ceasefire viability with both sides seeking leverage
Live updates on missile waves and Trump's call to stop
Highlighted tensions with US and strike on Beirut after Hezbollah rockets
Reported Trump's claim of immediate ceasefire talks

headlines.title: Armenian PM Pashinyan's party wins election, cementing Westward shift
headlines.summary: Nikol Pashinyan's Civil Contract party won 49.81% of the vote with 59% turnout. The result confirms Armenia's turn toward the West despite Moscow's threats and claims of interference.

framings.angle: Focused on preliminary results and vote share
Emphasized geopolitical significance of Westward tilt

headlines.title: Goldman-backed Go prices Japan's biggest 2026 IPO at 2,400 yen per share
headlines.summary: Shareholders in the Goldman Sachs Group-backed firm sold shares at ¥2,400 each, marking Japan's largest initial public offering of 2026.

headlines.title: Nigerian army frees 360 hostages from Boko Haram mountain hideout
headlines.summary: Nigeria's army rescued 360 people taken from different communities by Boko Haram militants. Kidnappings remain a growing security concern across the country.

framings.angle: Reported rescue as part of broader security issues

headlines.title: Zelensky's European allies set out five conditions for peace talks with Russia
headlines.summary: Ukrainian President Zelensky met with close European allies to outline five conditions for peace talks, as US attention shifts to the Iran conflict.

headlines.title: South Africa unveils comprehensive plan to tackle illegal immigration
headlines.summary: The government adopted a Comprehensive Approach for Migration Management to strengthen border security, enforce laws, and close policy loopholes.

headlines.title: Peruvians vote in runoff election for ninth president in 10 years
headlines.summary: Voters chose between rightwing Keiko Fujimori and leftist Roberto Sánchez amid rising crime, corruption, and political instability.
headlines.previousContext: Peru held first-round elections in April; Fujimori led with 17% of votes. The runoff follows years of political turmoil.
